name: NI-HPC / Kelvin-2
oneLiner: Northern Ireland's flagship shared HPC service, mixing CPU scale with a broad GPU estate for UK research access.
summary: Kelvin-2 is a Tier-2 HPC facility jointly run by Queen's University Belfast and Ulster University. Installed in 2022, it combines 102 Dell PowerEdge R6525 nodes with high-memory partitions, 2 PB of Lustre storage, and a varied accelerator mix including NVIDIA V100, A100, H100, AMD MI300X, and Intel Max 1100 GPUs. This diversity makes it useful for inference experimentation and data-intensive workloads while also serving as a regional access point for UK researchers outside Northern Ireland.

keyFacts.hardware: 102 Dell PowerEdge R6525 nodes with AMD EPYC CPUs plus V100, A100, H100, MI300X, and Intel Max GPUs
keyFacts.performance: Tier-2 multi-architecture research platform with 2 PB Lustre storage
keyFacts.funding: Shared university platform with EPSRC-access allocation for part of capacity
keyFacts.energy: Shared regional infrastructure with pooled accelerator access

access.route: About 35% of resources are available free to researchers across the UK via EPSRC's HPC access process; the rest is reserved for local institutions.

useCases: Data-intensive inference experiments across different GPU vendors
Regional HPC training and public research support
